summary:
Positive intermittent vertical forces minimize lateral shear during orthodontic treatment; mechanical methods use elevation rather than direct pressure for safe tooth displacement.
The optimal technique involves gentle upward movements that separate roots without causing harm or unintended shifts elsewhere in the mouth.
\boxed{\text{Positive intermittent vertical forces}} Agent1's summary states:

Applying positive intermittent vertical forces is ideal as it mimics natural biting pressures and avoids damaging lateral shear forces. This method ensures safe tooth movement through gradual, controllable separation. \(\boxed{\text{Positive intermittent vertical forces}}\)
